agents.note: Session 121 -- US-245 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 20 8/10). E2E power-mgmt drain integration test wires US-242/US-243/US-244 modules (and US-216, US-225, US-234, US-235 dependencies) into a single drain-trace assertion. SHAPE: tests/pi/integration/test_power_mgmt_e2e_drain.py (NEW ~440 lines, 3 classes, 3 tests). Test #1 TestE2eDrainFiresFullPipeline: stair-step VCELL [4.20, 4.10, 3.90, 3.75, 3.60, 3.50, 3.46, 3.42] across 80s mocked time at 10s/step; mocks I2cClient.readWord (US-235 pattern) + subprocess.run inside shutdown_handler; uses REAL UpsMonitor + REAL PowerMonitor + REAL PowerDownOrchestrator + REAL ShutdownHandler with suppressLegacyTriggers=True (US-225 invariant). Asserts: UpsMonitor flips to BATTERY (US-235 sustained + slope rules; CRATE returns 0xFFFF disabled), power_log gains row(s) with on_ac_power=0/power_source=battery (US-243 fan-out wiring proof; Spool's 2026-04-29 drill found 8 transitions to journal but 0 to power_log because PowerMonitor was never instantiated -- this test pins the path), orchestrator state==TRIGGER with stage callback order [warning, imminent] (TRIGGER has no callback per US-216), battery_health_log row populated with start_soc=4.10V (highest pre-WARNING VCELL on battery; t=10s tick where source first flipped) + end_soc=3.42V + populated end_timestamp + runtime_seconds, subprocess.run called once with ['systemctl','poweroff']. Test #2 TestE2eRecoveryCancelsDrain: drain-into-WARNING then VCELL recovers to 4.10V flat; orchestrator returns to NORMAL via either AC-restore OR hysteresis path (the production case hits hysteresis FIRST because UpsMonitor's slope rule keeps source BATTERY while drain samples age out of 60s rolling window). Asserts safety INVARIANT (state==NORMAL + zero poweroff calls + battery_health_log row closed) WITHOUT pinning which recovery path fired. Test #3 TestE2eSocPinnedDoesNotPreventTrigger: bug-class proof. SOC pinned 60% throughout the drain (the 4-drain production failure mode US-234 fixed); full chain still triggers correctly proving the post-US-234 VCELL ladder is the load-bearing path. (3) Initial test had over-specific onAcRestore callback assertion; rewrote to assert safety invariant (NORMAL state + zero poweroff calls + drain row closed) rather than which recovery path fired. Leads with a 'Schema Impact' gate as a 3-checkbox question (server migration required: yes/no/N/A). Documents why it exists with a TD-039 backlink. (c) CI SCHEMA-DIFF: scripts/schema_diff.py (NEW ~310 lines). loadPiSchema() executes the canonical CREATE TABLE constants from src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py (ALL_SCHEMAS) + 6 sibling schema modules (drive_summary, dtc_log_schema, pi_state, sync_log, calibration.types, power.battery_health) in an in-memory sqlite3 connection -- column extraction delegated to PRAGMA table_info (no fragile regex). loadServerSchema() walks SQLAlchemy Base.metadata.tables. computeDiff() is pure -- takes plain dicts, returns deterministic JSON-shaped dict (sort-stable ordering for CI compatibility). main() emits JSON to stdout, exits 0 if no Pi-only-drift in shared tables, 1 if drift detected. TWO LOAD-BEARING DESIGN DECISIONS: (1) Gate trips ONLY on the TD-039 silent-data-loss direction (Pi has columns server lacks, not the inverse). Server-side extras (analytics columns, PK strategy diffs) are reported in sharedTableDrift for visibility but do NOT fail the gate -- they don't risk data loss and would otherwise drown CI in by-design noise (drive_summary/profiles/vehicle_info would always trip). (2) Two allowlists keep the gate signal-only: SERVER_MIRROR_COLUMNS (source_id/source_device/synced_at/sync_batch_id -- US-CMP-003 sync convention) and PI_PK_RENAMED_TO_ID (battery_health_log.drain_event_id and calibration_sessions.session_id -- documented in producing modules' Sync shape blocks; the sync client renames Pi PK -> server id on the wire). SHAPE: UpdateApplier class (src/pi/update/update_applier.py NEW ~470 lines) with constructor DI for subprocessRun (default subprocess.run) + 3 fail-OPEN safety closures (isDrivingFn, getPowerSourceFn, getLastObdActivitySecondsAgoFn). 13 typed terminal outcomes via ApplyOutcome StrEnum (SUCCESS / NO_MARKER / MARKER_INVALID / DISABLED / SKIPPED_DRIVING / SKIPPED_BATTERY_POWER / SKIPPED_RECENT_OBD_ACTIVITY / DRY_RUN_FAILED / DEPLOY_FAILED / POST_DEPLOY_VERIFY_FAILED / ROLLBACK_OK / ROLLBACK_FAILED / CONFIG_ERROR). The method NEVER raises -- every error path resolves to a typed outcome. DECISION ORDER (load-bearing): (1) marker presence/shape -- malformed markers auto-cleared so a poisoned target version cannot perma-trigger; (2) safety gates: drive-state (sacred, highest priority) -> power source (BATTERY blocks to avoid dirty mid-shutdown) -> recent OBD activity (300s hardcoded -- engine just turned on but detector hasn't fired yet); gates check BEFORE the applyEnabled gate so the operator log shows 'would have been safe to apply' instead of hiding under the disabled flag; (3) applyEnabled gate (CIO opt-in safety net -- defaults FALSE so production never auto-applies until CIO flips the flag); (4) capture priorRef via 'git rev-parse HEAD' (failure -> CONFIG_ERROR, no state mutation); (5) git fetch -> checkout target -> deploy-pi.sh --dry-run -> deploy-pi.sh full -> verify post-deploy .deploy-version.version == target. ANY phase failure invokes _maybeRollback(priorRef): when rollbackEnabled=true runs 'git checkout priorRef' + 'sudo systemctl restart eclipse-obd'; ALWAYS clears marker (atomicity invariant -- success or rollback or rollback-skipped, marker always gone). FAIL-OPEN INVARIANT: missing or raising safety closures default to 'safe to apply' -- the load-bearing safety net is applyEnabled=false; per-gate fail-open ensures a broken UpsMonitor / detector / DB cannot perma-block updates indefinitely. ORCHESTRATOR WIRING: lifecycle._initializeUpdateApplier (gated on pi.update.enabled, soft-fail on construction error, mirrors _initializeUpdateChecker shape) constructs UpdateApplier with 3 lazy closures: (a) isDrivingFn lazy-resolves _driveDetector at call time (matches US-247 pattern); (b) getPowerSourceFn lazy-resolves HardwareManager.upsMonitor at call time, returns 'external' on missing component; (c) getLastObdActivitySecondsAgoFn runs SQL `SELECT (julianday('now')-julianday(MAX(timestamp)))*86400 FROM connection_log` against live DB, returns None on no rows / no DB / SQL error. core._maybeTriggerUpdateApply (parallel shape to _maybeTriggerUpdateCheck) gates FIRST on a CHEAP fast-path probe (UpdateApplier.markerExists() = single Path.is_file()) before doing anything else -- when no marker exists the runLoop pass adds essentially zero overhead. Cadence gate uses _updateCheckIntervalSeconds (interval symmetry with check); exception-isolated. runLoop calls it once per pass right after _maybeTriggerUpdateCheck. CONFIG: 3 new DEFAULTS (pi.update.applyEnabled=False [CIO opt-in], pi.update.stagingPath=/tmp/eclipse-obd-staging, pi.update.rollbackEnabled=True). config.json mirrors. SCOPE FENCE NOTES: (1) Story scope.filesToTouch listed only `tests/pi/update/test_update_applier.py`; added second test file `tests/pi/orchestrator/test_orchestrator_update_apply.py` parallel to Session 118 US-247's test_orchestrator_update_check.py shape -- pins runLoop wiring + closure-fail-open invariants which are load-bearing for the safety contract; structurally within story scope. (2) Story acceptance text says 'skip if connection_log shows recent OBD activity within last 5 min' -- 5min hardcoded as 300s constant in the applier (not config-driven); load-bearing for safety contract, promote to config only if vehicle tuning becomes necessary. (3) Story acceptance scope listed deploy/deploy-pi.sh as needing UPDATE for --dry-run flag verification; the flag already exists at deploy-pi.sh:117 from prior sprint work, no changes needed. Honored stopCondition #1 by inspecting first. (4) Rollback uses `git checkout priorRef` rather than `git stash apply prior` (story acceptance mentioned 'git stash old version, apply new'); equivalent semantics for a single-branch deploy without uncommitted changes, simpler to test and reason about, matches stagingPath isolation model. Last session: 'Session 118 -- US-247 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 20 5/10). B-047 US-C Pi update-checker daemon LIVE. SHAPE: UpdateChecker class (src/pi/update/update_checker.py NEW ~330 lines) with constructor DI for httpOpener (default urllib.request.urlopen) and isDrivingFn (zero-arg callable). Reads pi.update.* for policy (enabled, intervalMinutes, markerFilePath, localVersionPath); REUSES pi.companionService.baseUrl + apiKeyEnv for transport (single source of truth -- the Pi has one server it talks to, so one transport config). check_for_updates() returns a typed CheckResult dataclass(slots=True) carrying {outcome, localVersion, serverVersion, markerPath, rationale}. CheckOutcome StrEnum has 8 terminal states: UPDATE_AVAILABLE / UP_TO_DATE / SKIPPED_DRIVING / DISABLED / LOCAL_VERSION_UNAVAILABLE / SERVER_NO_RECORD / NETWORK_ERROR / CONFIG_ERROR. The method NEVER raises -- every error path resolves to a typed outcome so the runLoop caller has no try/except for expected failure modes (defensive Exception catch at outer boundary as last-resort). DECISION ORDER: drive-state gate (highest priority -- sacred) -> enabled gate -> API-key gate -> local .deploy-version readable gate -> server fetch -> validateRelease(server payload) -> parseVersion comparison -> marker write (only when server > local). SERVER 503 (US-246 contract for 'no record stamped') maps to SERVER_NO_RECORD (clean skip, no marker, no error log) -- Pi treats it as 'no update available' per acceptance contract. SERVER 5xx / network exception -> NETWORK_ERROR (transient; next interval retries). MARKER FILE shape: {target_version, server_url, rationale, checked_at} JSON; parent dir created via mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True); existing marker overwritten so the latest decision wins (US-D apply step is the only consumer). ORCHESTRATOR WIRING: lifecycle._initializeUpdateChecker (gated on pi.update.enabled, soft-fail on construction error) hands the checker an isDrivingFn closure that resolves _driveDetector LAZILY (defensive late-resolution -- detector reference can be re-bound by lifecycle paths) and returns False when detector absent (open-by-default safety: a missing detector must NEVER block all update checks indefinitely). core._maybeTriggerUpdateCheck (parallel shape to US-226's _maybeTriggerIntervalSync) gates on _updateCheckIntervalSeconds elapsed since _lastUpdateCheckTime, calls check_for_updates, exception-isolated. runLoop calls it once per pass right after _maybeTriggerIntervalSync. CONFIG: 4 new DEFAULTS (pi.update.enabled=true, pi.update.intervalMinutes=60, pi.update.markerFilePath=/var/lib/eclipse-obd/update-pending.json, pi.update.localVersionPath=.deploy-version). config.json mirrors. SCOPE FENCE NOTES: (1) story scope.filesToTouch listed `tests/pi/obdii/orchestrator/test_orchestrator_update_check.py` -- but `tests/pi/obdii/orchestrator/` does not exist. FOURTH session in a row hitting this drift (Sessions 114, 115, 116, 117 all noted). Actual orchestrator test directory is `tests/pi/orchestrator/`; placed integration test there. (2) Story acceptance text says 'Update-check skipped if drive_state != IDLE' but DriveState enum has no IDLE state (UNKNOWN/STOPPED/STARTING/RUNNING/STOPPING/ENDED). The invariant 'Drive-state-is-sacred: never check or apply during active drive' is the load-bearing spec; honored by gating on driveDetector.isDriving() (returns True only for RUNNING per detector.py:431). STARTING/STOPPING transitions are NOT gated (short-lived; check_for_updates is idempotent); for US-248 apply step the safety preconditions list is more stringent. (3) Story scope listed only core.py for runLoop wiring; placed the UpdateChecker init helper in lifecycle.py instead because that's where _initializeSyncClient + _initializePowerMonitor live and the 12-step init sequence is the canonical home for component lifecycle (matches Session 115 US-243 precedent). (4) Reused pi.companionService.baseUrl + apiKeyEnv for transport instead of duplicating into pi.update -- single source of truth for 'where the Pi talks to the server'; avoids drift between pi.companionService.baseUrl and pi.update.serverUrl. SHAPE: ReleaseReader dataclass(slots=True) with constructor DI for currentPath/historyPath/historyMaxEntries (golden DI pattern). readCurrent() delegates to scripts.version_helpers.readDeployVersion (existing US-241 file-read+validate; returns dict|None). readHistory() reads JSONL append trail, validates each line via version_helpers.validateRelease, silently skips malformed/blank lines (warning-logged), truncates to last N. fromSettings(settings) factory uses getattr fallbacks. ENDPOINTS: GET /api/v1/release/current returns the bare {version, releasedAt, gitHash, description} record (so US-247 Pi update-checker can read response.version directly without unwrapping); 503 when no record stamped.
